title: Writing a Strong Quality Agreement
alt: Five steps to draft and negotiate a quality technical agreement

items.label: Start from risk
items.desc: Draft from an ICH Q9 risk assessment, not a template.

items.label: Use a responsibility matrix
items.desc: An X-table giving every duty one accountable owner.

items.label: Name roles, not people
items.desc: Reference QP, RP or RPi so it survives staff changes.

items.label: Agree a review cycle
items.desc: Set a periodic review date and clear ownership.

items.label: Get real quality sign-off
items.desc: Signatories who own the obligations, not procurement.
